name: Dark Six, The
description: The malevolent or chaotic shadow-pantheon of Eberron, cast out from the Sovereign Host.
core_beliefs: The world is a harsh, predatory place. One must appease the gods of storms, slaughter, and betrayal to survive the shadows.
followers: Monsters, outcasts, survivalists, and those living in lawless frontiers.
history: Ancient myths claim they were purged from the Sovereign Host for refusing to adhere to the laws of civilization.
philosophy: Survival of the fittest; the necessity of acknowledging the 'dark half' of existence as a fundamental truth.
mythology: The Shadow, the Fury, and the Devourer represent the raw, untamed forces of nature and the psyche.

name: Faerûnian Pantheon, The
description: The primary religious system of the Forgotten Realms (Toril), involving dozens of deities managing cosmic 'portfolios.'
core_beliefs: The gods are active participants in reality. Every mortal must choose a patron or face a grim fate in the afterlife (The Wall of the Faithless).
followers: Humans, Half-Elves, and most urbanized civilizations in Faerûn.
history: Marked by the 'Time of Troubles' (gods walked as mortals) and the 'Second Sundering' (realignment of the planes).
philosophy: The belief that divine favor is a tangible currency and that one's destiny is woven by cosmic powers.
mythology: The eternal conflict between Selûne (Moon) and Shar (Loss) over the light of the sun and the creation of the world.

name: Lords of the Nine, The (Asmodean Cults)
description: The worship of Asmodeus and the Archdevils who rule the Nine Hells of Baator.
core_beliefs: The universe is a chaotic mess that only absolute hierarchy and strict laws (backed by fear) can save.
followers: Tyrants, corrupt nobles, ambitious mages, and those seeking power through contracts.
history: Asmodeus, an ancient angelic being, fell to the Hells to bind the demons of the Abyss, eventually claiming godhood through legal maneuver.
philosophy: The strong have a moral right to exploit the weak through law; power is the only objective truth.
mythology: The Pact Primeval—the ultimate legal contract allowing devils to claim mortal souls in exchange for worldly power.

name: Morndinsamman, The (Dwarven Pantheon)
description: The 'Shield of the Dwarves,' a pantheon of deities led by Moradin the Soul Forger.
core_beliefs: Labor is prayer. Perfection in craftsmanship, absolute loyalty to clan, and honoring the ancestors are the paths to holiness.
followers: Dwarves of all subraces, smiths, and stonemasons.
history: Forged when Moradin created the first dwarves from the earth; defined by the defense of subterranean kingdoms.
philosophy: Traditionalism: The belief that social stability and quality of work are the only things that endure against time.
mythology: Moradin hammering the first dwarven souls into shape on the Anvil of the World in the planet's core.

name: Seldarine, The (Elven Pantheon)
description: The elven gods dwelling in the plane of Arvandor, led by Corellon Larethian.
core_beliefs: Life is a grand art form. Magic, artistic expression, and the freedom of the soul are the highest virtues.
followers: Elves of all subraces and Half-Elves.
history: Stemming from the blood of Corellon shed during the first war with Gruumsh; marked by the tragic betrayal of Lolth.
philosophy: The belief in the fluid nature of identity and the cycle of elven reincarnation as a path to perfection.
mythology: The creation of Evermeet, the island refuge for elves, and the eternal beauty of the Feywild.

name: Sovereign Host, The
description: The primary 'civilized' faith of Eberron, representing the pillars of society.
core_beliefs: The gods are omnipresent forces found in every act of creation, law, and harvest. They act through mortal hands.
followers: Citizens of the Five Nations across nearly all common races.
history: Evolved from the ancient myths of the Great Dragons creating the world after the Age of Demons.
philosophy: Communitarianism: Success is a collaborative effort between divine inspiration and human labor.
mythology: The Progenitor Myth: The world is the dragon Eberron, floating between Siberys (Heavens) and Khyber (Underdark).

name: Spider Queen, The (Cult of Lolth)
description: The oppressive, matriarchal religion of the Drow (Dark Elves).
core_beliefs: Only the strong, the cruel, and the cunning deserve to survive. Chaos is the natural order of the world.
followers: Drow living in the Underdark.
history: Lolth was cast out of the Seldarine for her treachery, leading her followers into the darkness to build a new empire.
philosophy: Survival of the Fittest: Betrayal is a virtue if it leads to an increase in personal or family status.
mythology: The 'Silence of Lolth'—periods where the goddess tests her followers' faith by withdrawing her power.

name: Athar, The (The Defiers)
description: A faction centered in Sigil that rejects the divinity of all gods.
core_beliefs: The 'gods' are just powerful mortals ('Powers') who don't deserve worship. True divinity is an unreachable 'Great Unknown.'
followers: Scholars, skeptics, and those disillusioned by religious wars.
history: Rose to prominence in the City of Doors by proving that gods can die and fade away.
philosophy: Rationalism: Relying on the self and the laws of the multiverse rather than divine whim.
mythology: The 'Shattered Temple'—the belief that all religious structures are monuments to vanity.

name: Bleak Cabal, The (Bleakers)
description: A faction that believes the universe has no inherent meaning or plan.
core_beliefs: The multiverse is indifferent to suffering. Therefore, meaning must be created by the individual through acts of mercy.
followers: Philanthropists, the mentally ill, and existential philosophers.
history: Headquartered in Sigil's Madhouse, where they provide care for the forgotten and downtrodden.
philosophy: Because nothing matters, the only thing that *can* matter is the kindness we show to each other in the void.
mythology: The 'Grim Retreat'—the internal journey to find peace in a meaningless world.

name: Blood of Vol, The (Seekers)
description: A faith that rejects the afterlife and gods in favor of the 'Divinity Within.'
core_beliefs: Death is a void. The gods are parasites. We must seek immortality or godhood within our own blood and community.
followers: Karrnathi humans and those who view undeath as a tool to protect the living.
history: Founded by the lich Erandis Vol after the purging of the Mark of Death line.
philosophy: Self-Divinity: The spark of godhood is inside you; to die is to lose your only chance at true existence.
mythology: The myth of the 'Crimson Covenant'—the preservation of life through blood and necromancy.

name: Cult of Elemental Evil, The
description: Apocalyptic cults dedicated to the four Princes of Elemental Evil.
core_beliefs: The current world is a prison. It must be razed by the raw elements (Fire, Water, Earth, Air) to be purified.
followers: Nihilists, madmen, and corrupted elemental beings.
history: Famously defeated at the Temple of Elemental Evil, yet constantly reborn in different forms.
philosophy: Destruction as a means of 'cleansing' the world for the return of the Elder Elemental Eye.
mythology: The release of Tharizdun, the Chained God, whose awakening marks the end of all matter.

name: Emerald Enclave, The
description: A widespread organization of druids and rangers dedicated to the 'Great Balance.'
core_beliefs: The natural order must be protected from both the over-expansion of civilization and the ravages of evil.
followers: Druids, Rangers, Barbarians, and wilderness dwellers.
history: Established on the island of Ilighôn to maintain equilibrium between nature and industry.
philosophy: Environmental Balance: Civilization and wilderness must coexist without one destroying the other.
mythology: The 'Great Balance'—the cosmic rhythm of life and death that must never be disrupted.

name: Fated, The (Takers)
description: A faction in Sigil that believes everyone gets exactly what they have the strength to take.
core_beliefs: Might makes right. If you can't hold something, you don't own it. Compassion is a drain on resources.
followers: Tax collectors, merchants, warlords, and social climbers.
history: Grew from the harsh merchant culture of the planes; they often control the halls of records and taxes.
philosophy: Self-Interest: The belief that the universe functions best when every individual pursues their own power.
mythology: The 'Taker's Creed'—the universe is a vault waiting for the strong to pick its lock.

name: Free League, The (Indeps)
description: A loosely organized group that rejects all formal factions and hierarchies.
core_beliefs: Individual freedom is the only true law. No god or government has the right to command a free soul.
followers: Rebels, frontier explorers, and independent traders.
history: Formed as a silent majority in Sigil who refused to pay taxes or follow faction mandates.
philosophy: Total Individualism: 'Think for yourself' as a moral obligation and political stance.
mythology: The Unbound Soul—the belief that the first beings were born without chains.

name: Harmonium, The (Hardheads)
description: A faction that seeks to achieve universal peace through absolute conformity to law.
core_beliefs: Conflict comes from disagreement. Therefore, if everyone follows the same law, there will be no conflict.
followers: City guards, soldiers, and paladins of extreme order.
history: Originated on the plane of Ortho, where they successfully converted the entire population to a single mindset.
philosophy: Collectivism: The rights of the individual are secondary to the 'Harmony' of the state.
mythology: The 'Golden Age of One Mind'—a prophecy of a future where all thought is synchronized.

name: Harpers, The
description: A secretive network of spies and mages working for the common good and balance.
core_beliefs: Power corrupts; therefore, it must be decentralized. Freedom and knowledge are the birthrights of all.
followers: Bards, Wizards, Spies, and vigilantes.
history: Founded in Myth Drannor to protect the common folk from the rise of tyrants and lich-lords.
philosophy: Egalitarianism: Protecting the weak from the abuses of the powerful and ensuring the flow of information.
mythology: The Harper Code—a set of ethics emphasizing humility, vigilance, and the pursuit of justice.

name: Knights of Solamnia, The
description: The legendary chivalric order of the world of Krynn (Dragonlance).
core_beliefs: 'Est Sularus oth Mithas' (My Honor is my Life). Strict loyalty to the Oath and the Measure.
followers: Nobles, paladins, and warriors of Ansalon.
history: Founded by Vinas Solamnus to bring law to a chaotic world during the Age of Dreams.
philosophy: Chivalry: The belief that a warrior's soul is defined by their adherence to a code of honor and justice.
mythology: The sacrifice of Huma Dragonbane, who used the Dragonlances to drive back Tiamat.

name: Mercykillers, The (The Red Death)
description: A faction in Sigil obsessed with the absolute execution of justice.
core_beliefs: Mercy is a crime. Justice is a machine that must run without emotion. All crimes must be punished.
followers: Executioners, bounty hunters, and inquisitors.
history: Formed from the merger of the Sons of Mercy and the Sodkillers to form the ultimate wing of the law.
philosophy: The belief that only through suffering and punishment can a soul be cleansed of its transgressions.
mythology: The Scales of Vengeance—the internal discipline to kill one's own mercy.

name: Order of the Gauntlet, The
description: A militant association of faithful warriors who hunt evil proactively.
core_beliefs: Evil must be confronted where it stands. Faith is both a shield and a weapon for the righteous.
followers: Paladins and Clerics of Torm, Helm, and Tyr.
history: Formed as a coalition to respond to rising supernatural threats across Faerûn.
philosophy: The moral imperative to intervene; believing that passive goodness is an invitation to evil.
mythology: The 'Triad'—the combined virtues of Duty (Torm), Justice (Tyr), and Perseverance (Ilmater).

name: Path of Light, The (Il-Yannah)
description: A psionic philosophy followed by the Kalashtar to combat the Dreaming Dark.
core_beliefs: The current age is one of nightmares. By changing the internal light of the soul, we change the age itself.
followers: Kalashtar and the people of Adar.
history: Formed when rebel spirits from the Plane of Dreams fled to Eberron and merged with human monks.
philosophy: Mindful Pacifism: The world is transformed through meditation and collective kindness, not war.
mythology: The 'Great Turning of the Age'—the cosmic struggle for the soul of the Plane of Dreams.

name: Silver Flame, The
description: A dominant faith in Eberron centered on a divine force of pure light and goodness.
core_beliefs: The Flame is a divine power that binds ancient evils. Adherents must purge the darkness in the world and in themselves.
followers: Humans (Thrane), paladins, and demon-hunters.
history: Manifested when Tira Miron sacrificed herself to bind a demon lord, becoming the Voice of the Flame.
philosophy: Goodness is a duty. One must actively defend the innocent from supernatural and moral threats.
mythology: The sacrifice of the Couatls, who gave their lives to create the Silver Flame in the Age of Demons.

name: Zhentarim, The (The Black Network)
description: A mercenary guild and shadow network seeking to control trade through influence and force.
core_beliefs: Wealth is power. Stability is achieved through a monopoly on resources and commerce.
followers: Mercenaries, merchants, and assassins.
history: Originally a cult of Bane (Tyranny), it evolved into a secular corporate power focused on market dominance.
philosophy: Machiavellianism: The ends justify the means; power through trade is more stable than power through steel.
mythology: The 'Iron Ledger'—the belief that every man has a price and every debt must be paid.
